title: Ensure IAM users are members of access groups and IAM  policies are assigned only to access groups

input_row.title: Ensure IAM users are members of access groups and IAM  policies are assigned only to access groups
input_row.description: Simplify and secure the access management process by using access groups when you assign access to groups of users with identical access needs.
input_row.rationale: You can create an access group so that you can organize a set of users and service IDs into a single entity that makes it easy for you to assign access. You can assign a single policy to the group instead of assigning the same access multiple times for each individual user or service ID.
input_row.audit: Console: Audit user access policies: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access (IAM). 3. Click Users and select a user by clicking on the username. 4. Click Access Policies. ***If the user has individual access policies and you wish to remove them, complete the following steps. 5. Locate the row containing the access policy you wish to remove. Click the actions icon corresponding to that row and click Remove. API:N/A
input_row.remediation: Assign an access policy to an Access Group: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access(IAM). 3. Click Access Groups. 4. Click an Access Group name, then click the Access Policies tab 5. Click Assign access. Add members to an Access Group: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access(IAM). 3. Click Access Groups. 4. Click an Access Group name. 5. Click Add Users. Delete an access policy for a user: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access(IAM). 3. Click Users. 4. Click on a user name. 5. Click the Access Policies tab 6. Locate the row containing the access policy you wish to remove. Click the actions icon corresponding to that row and click Remove.
input_row.impact: Using access groups reduces the number of policies that must be created and managed in the account and simplifies the process of adding new users or service IDs or modifying user and service IDs access.
input_row.default_value: By default, only the Public Access Group is enabled in IBM Cloud accounts.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control analysis identifies that IBM Cloud APIs can be leveraged for querying IAM settings related to access groups and user policies, making this control verifiable through API calls.
- The audit methodology provided in the "audit" field includes detailed steps that, while manual, imply the potential for automation, as it only mentions non-API methods indirectly.
- Access policies and user assignments can be managed through IBM Cloud CLI and API calls, indicating their technical exposure.
- Given the nature of the control, which deals directly with IAM features (access groups and policies), it aligns with automated audit capabilities.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_iam_user_access_group_policies_assigned

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Utilize the following IBM Cloud CLI commands:
  - `ibmcloud iam users` to list IAM users and their associated access policies.
  - `ibmcloud iam access-groups` to list access groups and their policies, and to verify if users are members of any groups.
  
- API calls can be as follows:
  - Use `GET /v1/users` to fetch all users and their attributes.
  - Use `GET /v1/access_groups` to retrieve access groups and their associated policies.
  
- Specific resources to enumerate include:
  - All IAM users across the IBM Cloud account.
  - All access groups in the account.

- Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:
  - Ensure each IAM user has no direct policy assignments, and every user’s permissions are managed through access groups.

- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** All IAM users have their access managed solely by being part of one or more access groups, with no individual policies assigned.
  - **Fail:** Any IAM user has individual policies assigned directly to them.

- Example check:
  Use commands and API endpoints to ensure user policies are aligned with access group policies, confirming compliance with the control requirement.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively audited using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI commands. The use of these tools aligns with the capabilities for querying IAM settings related to access groups and user policies, making automation feasible and appropriate.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are technically correct, leveraging IBM Cloud CLI commands and API calls to list IAM users and access groups. The commands `ibmcloud iam users` and `ibmcloud iam access-groups` are valid for retrieving necessary information.
- The API endpoints `GET /v1/users` and `GET /v1/access_groups` are appropriate for fetching user and access group data.
- The program name follows a reasonable convention but could be more descriptive to indicate the specific compliance check being performed.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name `ibm_iam_user_access_group_policies_assigned` could be improved for clarity. A suggestion would be `ibm_iam_user_access_group_policy_compliance` to better reflect the compliance check aspect.
- Consider including additional checks for nested access groups, if applicable, to ensure comprehensive coverage.
- It might be beneficial to include error handling and logging in the automation script to capture any anomalies or issues during execution.
- Explore using IBM Cloud Config for continuous monitoring and compliance checks as an alternative or complementary approach.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-aligned with IBM Cloud's capabilities and best practices for IAM management. The use of automated tools and APIs is appropriate for this control, and the technical details provided are accurate.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `ibm_iam_user_access_group_policy_compliance` for clarity and descriptiveness.
- Keep the automated approach as it effectively utilizes IBM Cloud's capabilities for compliance verification.
